# FIXED, COMPLETE toolset — pre-installed and Docker-layer-cached.
#
# DO NOT edit this file or add libraries. The grader builds your image
# on every submission; this dependency layer is cached ONLY while
# requirements.txt is byte-for-byte unchanged. Any edit forces a full
# multi-minute reinstall on EVERY submission. Everything you may need
# for S1–S8 is already here — use only these.
#
# CPU-only: the index line pulls the CPU PyTorch wheel (no nvidia/CUDA).
--extra-index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cpu

# Web service the grader builds + runs.
fastapi==0.115.0
uvicorn==0.30.6
pydantic==2.9.2
httpx==0.28.1

# Retrieval / reliability (green bar reachable with rank_bm25 + numpy;
# sentence-transformers + faiss is the illustrative dense path — its
# model is baked into the image at build time, see Dockerfile).
numpy==1.26.4
rank_bm25==0.2.2
faiss-cpu==1.8.0.post1
torch==2.2.2+cpu
sentence-transformers==3.2.1

# Reliability + per-skill helpers (all pre-installed; use as needed):
tenacity==9.0.0          # retry/backoff -> graceful degradation (S7)
scrubadub==2.0.1         # S4 PII redaction (Apache-2.0, no model)
rapidfuzz==3.10.1        # S2 fuzzy intent matching (MIT)
pytest==8.3.3            # your own local tests

# Intentionally NOT pre-installed (heavy spaCy/transformers model
# downloads would make submission builds slow, and none are required):
#   presidio-analyzer, llm-guard, langgraph, langfuse.
# regex/stdlib fully covers S5; plain history handling covers S6.
# Observability (Langfuse/OpenLLMetry) is taught as a CONCEPT only —
# the grader sandbox blocks egress; the LLM proxy's returned usage/cost
# is this course's concrete telemetry stand-in. Do not add these.
